privileged_info_summary: The task requires extracting the SST at 15.5°N, 320.5°E from the real NOAA OI SST v2 LTM dataset. Analysis of the actual netCDF file reveals the true SST profile at this grid coordinate peaks at ~27.5°C, exceeding the 26.5°C threshold in August, September, October, and November. CRITICALLY: The verifier logic incorrectly hardcodes an expected peak of 28.2°C (which corresponds to a completely different coordinate like 19.5°N 307.5°E) and checks 'abs(actual_peak - 28.2) <= 0.5'. A perfectly accurate agent execution yielding the true peak of ~27.5°C will fail this check (28.2 - 27.5 = 0.7 > 0.5) and lose 20 points for 'Coordinate Accuracy', resulting in a false-negative penalty.
